title: Today's Tech Brief: How small businesses can leverage AI
summary: A look at how small businesses can use AI, and an octopus-inspired startup bringing AI to the physical world.
imageAlt: Image from source article for How small businesses can leverage AI
imageCaption: How small businesses can leverage AI
imageSourceTitle: How small businesses can leverage AI

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to Today's Tech Brief. I am looking at some interesting developments in the world of AI and small businesses. Let's dive in.

First up, small businesses are getting a boost from AI. According to an article from MIT Technology Review, companies can leverage large language models, or LLMs, across various industries, from accounting to design to market research. This technology is becoming more accessible, which is great news for smaller players. It seems like LLMs can help level the playing field, allowing small businesses to compete with larger ones in terms of automation and data analysis.

Next, an octopus-inspired startup called Morph is bringing AI to the physical world. This Axios report caught my eye - who wouldn't want an octopus-inspired AI solution? Apparently, Morph is making waves with its innovative approach. The idea of applying AI to the physical world is fascinating, and I'm curious to see where this technology takes us.

Another interesting development is the potential for AI to enhance human capabilities. While not directly related to small businesses or Morph, it's clear that AI is going to change the way we work and interact with the world around us. From automating routine tasks to providing insights that can inform our decisions, AI is poised to have a significant impact.

My quick read: today's tech stories are less about one giant thunderclap and more about a lively weather pattern. A little platform drama here, a little artificial intelligence irony there, and suddenly the industry has enough material for a very caffeinated morning show.
